messages.content:
When hydrogen gas reacts with a halogen such as chlorine gas, the compound produced would be _____

an ionic compound
a covalent compound
a salt
an alkaline solution
a covalent compound

In which process is ammonia gas produced?

Electrolysis process
Cracking process
Haber’s process
Hydrogenation process
Haber's process

Which of the following is the best explanation as to why cesium is the most reactive alkali metal?

It has the largest atomic size leading to the weakest attraction to its valence electron
It has the greatest ionization energy leading to a high ability to lose its valence electron
Both (a) and (b)
Neither (a) nor (b)
It has the largest atomic size leading to the weakest attraction to its valence electron

Which of the following is the best explanation as to why lithium is the least reactive alkali metal?

The ionization energy of lithium is the highest of all alkali metals
The ionization energy of lithium is the lowest of all alkali metals
The relative high density of lithium compared to all other alkali metals
None of these
The ionization energy of lithium is the highest of all alkali metals

Choose the alkali metal that reacts least vigorously with water.

Li
Na
K
Rb
Li (Lithium)

Choose the element with the highest softness.

Li
Na
K
Rb
Rb (Rubidium)

Choose the element with the highest density.

Li
Na
K
Rb
Rb (Rubidium)

Choose the element with the highest melting point.

Li
Na
Rb
Cs
Li (Lithium)

Which group contains the most reactive metals?

Group 1A
Group 2A
Group 3A
Group 4A
Group 1A

he elements in this group are termed alkali metals.

Group 1A
Group 2A
Group 3A
Group 4A
Group 1A
